Milton, we love the collection. But I have to negotiate the price, your markup is high for our floor.
I understand. Could you tell me how much room you need on price?
My counteroffer is twelve percent off. In return, we would commit to a larger volume.
Twelve is a real concession to ask for. I was wondering if eight would work with that volume.
Let us compromise. Ten percent, and would you mind splitting it into three installments?
That is fair. Ten percent over three installments, let us not reach a deadlock over the last point. We can settle this today.
We have some leverage here, could you tell me what volume you can commit to?

Hi Milton, it is Diane from Halstead, following up on the necklace collection. I have spoken to my team, and I have to be direct with you. Your markup is a little high for our floor this season, so I would like to make a counteroffer. Could you do twelve percent off on the first order? In return, we are ready to commit to a larger volume, which gives you something solid to plan around. I know that is a real concession to ask for, so I am open to a compromise: perhaps a smaller discount, but spread the payment over three installments. I would rather not reach a deadlock over a few points, because I genuinely want to settle this and start selling your pieces. Have a think, and let me know what works. Talk soon.

Hey team, Milton here with a quick voice note after the call with Diane. I think we can close this one, but we have to negotiate it smartly. She asked for twelve percent off, which is steep, so my plan is a counteroffer of eight, framed politely: I was wondering if eight percent would work if they raise the volume. The leverage is on our side, because the collection is selling well elsewhere, so we do not have to give away the whole margin. I am happy to offer one concession on payment, the three installments, as long as they settle the rest on delivery. Let us avoid a deadlock by keeping the tone warm and the questions soft. If we meet in the middle on the discount, I think we have a fair bargain and a long-term partner. Let me know your thoughts before Friday.
